params.hours: int; window of N hours ending at ets (ets defaults to now-UTC). Verified: hours=2, hours=24.
params.sts: start timestamp, UTC ALWAYS. Parser is strptime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M') on value[:16] (seconds and 'Z'/offset suffix silently DISCARDED) or '%Y%m%d%H%M'. Verified: sts=2026-06-09T21:00Z worked, returned valid range exactly 21:00Z->21:00Z.
params.ets: end timestamp, same parsing as sts
params.wfos: comma-separated 3-4 char WFO ids, e.g. wfos=DMX,DVN. Verified filters correctly WITH sts/ets. CAUTION: with hours= (no ets) responses are memcached 60s under a key that OMITS wfos -> you can get another request's unfiltered/different-filtered cached payload. Always pair wfos with explicit sts+ets.
params.wfo: singular; DOES NOT FILTER the default listing (verified: wfo=LOT&hours=24 returned 48 WFOs). Only used in the VTEC lookup mode (phenomena/significance/eventid/year).
params.states: comma-separated 2-char state abbrs; verified states=IL returns only IL. Uses ST_Intersects against state polygons. SIDE EFFECT: this code path leaks an extra property 'l' containing the raw Postgres composite row as a string (server-side SQL typo 'l,county'). Ignore unknown keys.
params.west_east_north_south: decimal-degree bbox; ALL FOUR required or HTTP error. Verified returns only points inside box (ST_Contains). OVERRIDES wfos if both given (code replaces the wfos limiter).
params.phenomena+significance+eventid+year+wfo: VTEC mode: LSRs spatially/temporally within one storm-based warning. Output in this mode LACKS product_id.
params.inc_ap: bool; adds 'prodlinks' HTML property of associated warnings (slow; per-row queries)
params.no_time_params: silently returns empty FeatureCollection (verified). sts without ets: also silently empty. Must send hours OR (sts AND ets).

primary_endpoint.row_limit: LIMIT 10000 (default and states paths; from source). 24h national on 2026-06-10 = 996 features / ~494 KB, so national fetch is fine.
primary_endpoint.feature_id: feature 'id' is the stringified dataframe row index ('0','1',...) - NOT stable; dedupe on (product_id, valid, lon, lat, type, magnitude).
primary_endpoint.history: lsrs table has data back to ~2002 (quality good from 2005/2006 per IEM docs)

properties_schema_observed.typetext: str uppercase event text, e.g. 'TSTM WND GST'
properties_schema_observed.magnitude: STRING (legacy); '' when null, else str(magf) with trailing '.0' stripped, e.g. '60', '1.75'
properties_schema_observed.unit: str or null; observed: 'MPH'/'mph', 'Inch'/'inch' (CASE VARIES - compare case-insensitively)
properties_schema_observed.qualifier: 'M'=measured, 'E'=estimated, 'U'=unknown, null when no magnitude. Counts in 24h sample: M=656, null=269, E=69, U=2
properties_schema_observed.city: str location, may be relative like '2 SW Williamstown'
properties_schema_observed.source: str report source; top observed: Mesonet, Public, Emergency Mngr, Trained Spotter, ASOS, Law Enforcement, AWOS, Dept of Highways
properties_schema_observed.remark: str free text or null (never empty string in 24h sample: 758 text / 238 null)
properties_schema_observed.product_id: str like '202606102124-KLOT-NWUS53-LSRLSR-id', coalesce(product_id_summary, product_id); absent in VTEC mode

M.magnitude: MPH not knots (verified vs remark '40 mph/35 kt' -> magnitude 40)

2.typetext: DUST STORM or BLOWING DUST (one code, two typetexts)

type_codes_observed_24h_2026-06-10.note: codes are season-dependent; winter adds S=SNOW (inch), 5=ICE STORM etc. One code can map to multiple typetexts. Match on type for class, keep typetext for display.

alternate_endpoint_point_radius.params: lon (required, deg E), lat (required, deg N), radius_degrees (<10) OR radius_miles (<1000), begints / endts (UTC ISO date-time)
schema_differences_vs_geojson_service: magnitude is a NUMBER (62.0) or null - not a string
top-level has name and crs keys
this is the only documented endpoint in /api/1/docs; the /geojson/lsr.geojson service is the self-documented legacy app (docs at https://mesonet.agron.iastate.edu/geojson/lsr.py?help)

fixture_provenance.iem_lsr_sample.geojson: raw features from GET /geojson/lsr.geojson?sts=2026-06-09T21:00:00Z&ets=2026-06-10T21:00:00Z (996 national features), stratified-trimmed to 50 features: all 6 tornadoes + round-robin across the other 11 type codes; feature objects byte-identical to received
fixture_provenance.iem_lsr_bypoint_sample.geojson: raw, untrimmed response from the lsrs_by_point example above (52 features)
